title: pinguicula pygmaea
intro: Pinguicula pygmaea, also known as the pygmy butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species native to North America, belonging to the genus Pinguicula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, Pinguicula pygmaea is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely anecdotal and not widely recognized in mainstream medicine.
